medical-research-mode.activation: This mode activates when the content contains: - Clinical claims with statistics (p-values, hazard ratios, confidence intervals) - Gene names, variants, or genomic coordinates - Drug names, dosages, or treatment protocols - Disease names with clinical context - References to clinical trials or studies

claim-types.type: Claims involving p-values, odds ratios, hazard ratios, AUC values - Verify: Are statistics correctly interpreted? - Check: Sample size adequate for claims? - Note: Confidence intervals if available
Claims about biological mechanisms, pathways, gene function - Verify: Supported by primary literature? - Check: Consistent with established knowledge? - Note: Any emerging/contested findings
Claims about diagnosis, treatment, prognosis - Verify: Evidence level (RCT, cohort, case series)? - Check: Guideline concordance? - Note: Limitations and contraindications
Claims about variants, gene-disease associations - Verify: ClinVar classification if available - Check: Population frequency (gnomAD) - Note: Functional evidence status

variant-interpretation.acmg-criteria: Reference ACMG/AMP criteria when discussing variant pathogenicity: - PVS1: Null variant in gene where LOF is mechanism - PS1-PS4: Strong evidence of pathogenicity - PM1-PM6: Moderate evidence - PP1-PP5: Supporting evidence - BA1, BS1-BS4: Benign evidence - BP1-BP7: Supporting benign evidence
variant-interpretation.databases: Relevant databases for verification: - ClinVar: Clinical significance - gnomAD: Population frequencies - OMIM: Gene-disease relationships - UniProt: Protein function - dbSNP: Variant identifiers

genomics-specific.ngs-quality: When reviewing NGS results, consider: - Coverage depth (typically >30x for germline, >100x for somatic) - Variant allele frequency context - Quality scores (QUAL, GQ, DP) - Mapping quality and alignment issues - Potential technical artifacts

endpoints.primary: - OS: Overall Survival - PFS: Progression-Free Survival - ORR: Overall Response Rate - DFS: Disease-Free Survival
endpoints.secondary: - DoR: Duration of Response - TTR: Time to Response - QoL: Quality of Life measures - Safety/tolerability

clinical-trial-context.regulatory-considerations: - FDA approval status - EMA considerations - Off-label use implications - Companion diagnostic requirements

medical-research-mode.output-format:
When medical/clinical content is detected, include:
### Clinical Verification
**Claims Analyzed:** - [Claim 1]: [Verification status] - [Source/Evidence] - [Claim 2]: [Verification status] - [Source/Evidence]
**Evidence Level:** [Grade based on study type]
**Caveats:** - [Any limitations or considerations]
**Recommended Reading:** - [Relevant guidelines or key papers if grounding available]

safety-notes.disclaimer: This analysis is for research purposes only and does not constitute medical advice. Clinical decisions should be made by qualified healthcare professionals based on individual patient circumstances.
safety-notes.red-flags: Flag for human review if content suggests: - Immediate patient safety concerns - Potential adverse drug reactions - Contraindicated combinations - Urgent clinical scenarios
